name: Taylor Jousters
description: The Taylor Street Jouster Nation was a European-American (Italian/Mexican) Chicago street gang that started on the Near West Side at Taylor and Oakley in Little Italy around 1960. Named after the medieval sport of jousting. Symbols: knight's helmet, two crossed lances, cross with four slashes. Allied with the Chicago Outfit and Gaylords. North side branches formed 1969-70 joined the WPO (White Power Organisation) and UFO (United Five Organisation) with Gaylords, Playboys, C-Notes, and Ventures. The original Taylor Street members (mostly Mexican by then) rejected the north side's white power stance. Most sections closed by mid-1980s; last active sets (Hanson Park, 46th/Talman) lasted until ~1996. Fully inactive by 2005.
